summary: Russian FPV drones struck a group near Chasiv Yar during the ceasefire, killing three soldiers later identified as Russian troops hit by their own side during an evacuation attempt. The Rubicon Center’s air defense crews used FPV drones to intercept over 2,000 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with operators accounting for more than 80 models, 75% of which belonged to just 12 types. On April 11, 2026, Rubicon operators conducted combat operations in the Donbas, Belgorod, and Krasny Liman directions, destroying 110 targets including Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs and personnel while coordinating with the Russian Aerospace Forces. Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to kill two Russian soldiers assaulting positions on motorcycles and to eliminate a Russian soldier who attempted to feign surrender before fleeing. In Kostiantynivka, Ukrainian soldiers survived a Russian FPV drone attack after detecting the threat with a drone detector. A Ukrainian EW system was also reported to interfere with FPV drones, forcing them to land within 15 seconds.
